team.detail: Roles are appropriate for tax specialist work in an AI-first model. Tax Specialist provides domain expertise, Engagement Partner and Manager provide governance, AI Engineer supports tooling, and Component Partner covers multi-jurisdiction oversight. In a traditional Big 4 engagement for Apple, the tax team would likely include 3-5 tax specialists plus staff generating 500-1000+ hours. The Superaudit model condenses this to ~108 human hours of review/oversight with AI performing the analytical heavy lifting ($200 AI cost for ETR analysis, deferred tax rollforwards, transfer pricing benchmarking, GILTI/FDII/BEAT calculations, and Pillar Two modeling). This is a reasonable AI-first design.

allocation.detail: Hours check: Tax Specialist 1×0.1×13wk×40h=52h, Engagement Partner 1×0.05×13×40=26h, Engagement Manager 1×0.03×13×40=15.6h, AI Engineer 1×0.02×13×40=10.4h, Component Partner 1×0.008×13×40=4.16h. Total ~108h, consistent with the described 100h (80h central + 20h component). The described human hour breakdown (specialist review 40h, UTP evaluation 20h, transfer pricing methodology 20h, component tax oversight 20h) maps well to these allocations. Component Partner at 4.16h for 40+ jurisdictions is light but defensible given AI performs the initial compliance reviews and the human role is exception-based oversight.

description.detail: Description is technically accurate. ASC 740-10 is the correct codification for uncertain tax positions (recognition and measurement). The €13B EU state aid reference is factual — the European Court of Justice upheld the ruling in September 2024 requiring Apple to repay €13B to Ireland, making it directly relevant to FY25 transfer pricing analysis. Apple's uncertain tax positions of ~$22B is consistent with recent 10-K disclosures (gross unrecognized tax benefits have been in the $19-22B range). GILTI/FDII/BEAT and Pillar Two are all relevant US and international tax provisions for Apple's structure. The 40+ jurisdictions claim is plausible given Apple's global operations across 26+ countries with multiple entities. No PCAOB AS paragraph numbers are cited, so none to verify as incorrect.

requirements.detail: specialist_evaluation is the correct and sufficient requirement_id for this workflow, mapping to AS 1210 (Using the Work of an Auditor-Engaged Specialist). The tax specialist's work product (TaxConclusions) feeds into the broader audit but the substantive testing of tax balances and related controls would be mapped in other workflows. No additional requirement_ids from the valid registry are clearly missing.

timing.detail: Weeks 6-18 (13 weeks) is well-timed. With Apple's FY ending September 27 and a 23-week engagement starting ~early October, week 6 falls in mid-November — after scoping/planning is complete and financial data is available. The workflow takes ReviewedScopingOutput as input, which would be ready by then. Ending at week 18 (~mid-February) provides adequate time before the 10-K filing deadline (~late November 60-day deadline, but the engagement timeline suggests interim and year-end work). The 13-week duration is appropriate for the described scope of ETR analysis, deferred tax rollforward, transfer pricing, UTP evaluation, GILTI/FDII/BEAT, and Pillar Two assessment with AI assistance.
